senses.definition: a device used to inflate sports balls, such as soccer balls and basketballs, by forcing air into them

examples.x: Don't forget to pack the ball pump for practice today.
The coach lent me his ball pump to inflate the volleyball before the game.
She quickly grabbed the ball pump to add more air to the basketball.
We need a new ball pump; this one isn't working properly anymore.
He used the ball pump to inflate the soccer ball to the correct pressure.

senses.definition: a wireless communication device allowing two or more people to communicate over a shared frequency

examples.x: The security team relied on their two-way radios to coordinate their movements around the building.
The workers used a two-way radio to communicate with each other across the construction site.
During the hike, we carried two-way radios to stay in touch in case we got separated.
At the theme park, staff members communicated using two-way radios to keep everything running smoothly.

senses.definition: a device used to measure the distance from the observer to a target

examples.x: The golfer used a rangefinder to determine the distance to the flag.
During the hunting trip, he relied on his rangefinder to gauge the distance to the deer.
Every marksman carries a rangefinder to accurately judge distances to their targets.
The golfer's rangefinder helped him select the right club for the shot based on distance.
The technician used a rangefinder to map out the dimensions of the building site.

senses.definition: a device that reduces vibrations or noise, making things more stable, comfortable, or effective

examples.x: The engineer used dampeners to reduce machine vibrations.
The cyclist put a dampener on the handlebars for a smoother ride.
Pilots rely on dampeners to steady the plane during turbulence.
The golf club's dampener helped absorb shock on mis-hits, improving accuracy.
The dampener on the bowstring reduced the sound and vibration of each arrow release.

senses.definition: a device used by cyclists to track and display various metrics such as speed, distance, and heart rate

examples.x: My cycling computer recorded my fastest speed yet on today's ride.
I rely on my cycling computer to monitor my heart rate during intense workouts.
The cycling computer beeped to remind me to hydrate after an hour of riding.
With a cycling computer, you can analyze your cycling performance with precise data.
The cycling computer's cadence sensor helps me maintain a steady pedal rhythm.

senses.definition: a safety device worn by athletes, primarily in motorsports like Formula 1 and NASCAR, to reduce the risk of neck and head injuries during crashes

examples.x: He strapped on his HANS device before getting into the race car.
Mechanics checked the fit of the HANS device to ensure it was secure.
The HANS device distributes crash forces away from the neck and head.

senses.definition: a safety mechanism that automatically initiates specific actions, such as deploying a parachute or releasing targets

examples.x: Competitive shooters use automatic activation devices to release clay targets at precise intervals.
Experienced skydivers and shooters rely on their automatic activation devices for safety and accuracy.
Before every jump or shooting round, athletes check their automatic activation devices meticulously.

senses.definition: a watch with a button to stop and start time, used in sport events

examples.x: The coach used a stopwatch to time each runner during the track practice.
She started the stopwatch as soon as the race began to measure her performance.
The scientist used a stopwatch to record the duration of a chemical reaction in the lab.
He reset the stopwatch after each trial to ensure accurate measurements in the experiment.
They used a digital stopwatch for precise timing in the competition.

senses.definition: a piece of sports equipment used in climbing to control the rope during belaying

examples.x: He attached the belay device to his harness before starting the climb.
She learned how to use a belay device to ensure safe climbing practices.
The new belay device features an innovative locking mechanism.
Before descending, he double-checked the setup of his belay device.
Climbers should regularly inspect their belay devices for wear and tear.

senses.definition: a device used in climbing to control or slow descent

examples.x: The climber attached the descender to the rope before rappelling down the cliff.
Make sure your descender is securely fastened to your harness before descending.
The new descender model features improved braking capabilities for safer descents.
The descender clicked smoothly as he descended down the vertical drop.
The instructor taught us different techniques for using the descender effectively.

senses.definition: the mechanism that connects the ski boot to the ski

examples.x: During the lesson, she learned how to release the binding for safety.
At the ski rental shop, they checked the bindings before fitting the boots.
The skier adjusted the binding to fit snugly with their ski boot.
Properly adjusted bindings are crucial for skiing safely and effectively.

senses.definition: a device used in fishing sports equipment that uses technology to locate fish underwater

examples.x: The fisherman used a fish finder to locate the best spot for casting his line.
The advanced fish finder displayed schools of fish swimming below the boat.
We relied on the fish finder to pinpoint the exact depth where the fish were congregating.
Using a fish finder dramatically increased our catch rate during the tournament.
The fish finder's screen lit up with colorful dots representing different fish sizes.

senses.definition: a specialized timepiece designed for underwater use, commonly used in scuba diving and snorkeling

examples.x: My new diving watch is waterproof up to 200 meters.
She wore a stylish diving watch with a stainless steel bracelet.
The instructor checked his diving watch before they descended.
This model of diving watch is known for its durability and accuracy.
The diving watch features luminous hands for visibility in dark waters.
